# How to Set Up Your Vendor Profile

Your vendor profile is your storefront on Fleet Feast. A complete, professional profile attracts more bookings and builds customer trust.

## Why Your Profile Matters

**First Impressions:**
- Customers judge within seconds
- Photos and descriptions sell your service
- Complete profiles rank higher in search
- Professional appearance builds trust

**Search Rankings:**
- Profiles with 10+ photos rank higher
- Complete profiles appear in more searches
- Featured vendors get prime placement
- Reviews and ratings boost visibility

**Booking Conversion:**
- Detailed profiles convert 3x better
- Clear pricing reduces questions
- Good photos increase booking requests
- Professional tone inspires confidence

## Accessing Your Profile

### After Approval

1. Log in to your vendor account
2. Click "Vendor Dashboard"
3. Select "Profile Settings"
4. Begin setup

### Profile Sections Overview

Your profile has 8 key sections:
1. Business Information
2. Photos & Media
3. About Your Business
4. Menu & Pricing
5. Service Details
6. Availability & Calendar
7. Policies & Requirements
8. Social Media & Links

## Step-by-Step Profile Setup

### 1. Business Information

**Required Fields:**
- **Business Name**: Your official truck name
- **Tagline**: Short, catchy description (e.g., "Authentic Street Tacos Since 2015")
- **Cuisine Type(s)**: Select all that apply
- **Service Area**: Cities/regions you serve
- **Base Location**: Your home city

**Optional but Recommended:**
- **Founded**: Year you started
- **Awards/Recognition**: Any notable achievements
- **Certifications**: Organic, sustainable, etc.

**Example:**
```
Business Name: Rollin' Smoke BBQ
Tagline: Award-Winning Texas BBQ on Wheels
Cuisine: BBQ, Southern, American
Service Area: Austin, Round Rock, San Marcos, Georgetown
Base Location: Austin, TX
Founded: 2018
Awards: Austin BBQ Festival - Best Brisket 2023
```

### 2. Photos & Media

**Cover Photo (Required):**
- Banner image at top of profile
- Dimensions: 1920x600px
- Shows truck in action or signature dish
- Professional, high-quality image

**Logo (Required):**
- Square format (500x500px minimum)
- Your business logo or truck icon
- Used in search results and messages
- Clear, recognizable design

**Truck Photos (Minimum 5, Recommended 10-15):**

**Essential Shots:**
1. Truck exterior (front view)
2. Truck exterior (side with service window)
3. Interior/kitchen area
4. Service window close-up
5. Setup at an event

**Additional Recommended:**
6. Truck at different events
7. Night shots (if you do evening events)
8. Truck decorated for special events
9. Team members serving customers
10. Truck in different seasons

**Food Photos (Minimum 10, Recommended 20-30):**

**Must-Have:**
- Each signature dish
- Close-ups showing detail
- Plated/presented beautifully
- Variety of menu items
- Popular combinations

**Pro Tips:**
✅ Natural lighting is best
✅ Clean, uncluttered backgrounds
✅ Show portion sizes
✅ Capture steam/freshness
✅ Include garnishes and sides
✅ Show variety

❌ Avoid dark, blurry photos
❌ Don't use stock photos
❌ Skip messy presentations
❌ Avoid photos with visible people (privacy)

**Event Photos (Recommended 5-10):**
- Your truck at weddings
- Corporate events
- Festivals
- Private parties
- Happy customers (with permission)
- Crowd shots showing popularity

**Video (Optional but Powerful):**
- 30-60 second highlight reel
- Show food preparation
- Customer testimonials
- Event montage
- Behind-the-scenes
- Upload to YouTube/Vimeo and link

### 3. About Your Business

**Business Description (Required, 200-500 words):**

Tell your story! Include:
- How you got started
- What makes you unique
- Your culinary philosophy
- Signature dishes
- What customers love
- Your mission/values

**Example:**
```
Rollin' Smoke BBQ was born from a passion for authentic Texas
barbecue. Owner and pitmaster Jake Thompson spent 15 years
perfecting his craft in the legendary BBQ joints of Austin before
launching his own food truck in 2018.

What sets us apart? We smoke our brisket for 16 hours over oak
and mesquite, never rushing the process. Our ribs have won awards
at the Austin BBQ Festival three years running, and our homemade
sauces (available mild to "Texas hot") complement without
overpowering the natural smoke flavor.

We're not just a food truck - we're a BBQ experience. Whether it's
a backyard birthday or a corporate event for 500, we bring the
same passion and quality to every plate. Our customers become
friends, and we love being part of their special moments.

From our family to yours, welcome to Rollin' Smoke BBQ!
```

**What Makes You Special (Optional, 100-200 words):**
- Unique selling points
- Awards and recognition
- Special techniques or ingredients
- Community involvement
- Sustainability practices

**Chef/Owner Bio (Optional, 100-300 words):**
- Your background
- Culinary training
- Experience
- Personal story
- Why you do what you do

### 4. Menu & Pricing

**Upload Current Menu:**
- PDF, image, or type directly
- Organize by categories
- Include descriptions
- Show prices
- Note dietary info

**Menu Categories:**
Structure for easy browsing:
- Appetizers
- Main Dishes
- Sides
- Desserts
- Beverages
- Specials

**Item Details:**

For each menu item:
- **Name**: What you call it
- **Description**: Ingredients, preparation, flavors
- **Price**: Individual or per-person
- **Dietary Tags**: Vegan, GF, vegetarian, etc.
- **Photo**: High-quality image
- **Portion Size**: "Serves 10-12" or "Individual serving"
- **Customization**: Options available

**Example Menu Item:**
```
🌮 Al Pastor Tacos
Marinated pork shoulder slow-cooked on a vertical spit with
pineapple, served on corn tortillas with cilantro, onion, and
lime. A customer favorite!

Price: $4.50 each or $40/dozen
Dietary: Can be made GF with corn tortillas
Spice Level: Medium (mild available)
Serves: 1 taco is a good portion, most guests order 2-3
```

**Pricing Structure:**

Choose what works for your business:

**Option 1: Per Person**
- Guest count × per person rate
- Include what's covered
- Example: "$15/person includes 2 tacos, chips, drink"

**Option 2: Package Pricing**
- Bronze, Silver, Gold tiers
- Different offerings at each level
- Clear value proposition

**Option 3: Flat Event Fee**
- Set price for the event
- Includes service time, food for X guests
- Travel fees separate

**Option 4: Custom Quotes**
- For complex events
- Requires consultation
- Flexible pricing

**Minimums:**
- Minimum guest count (e.g., "20 person minimum")
- Minimum booking fee (e.g., "$500 minimum charge")
- When/why minimums apply

### 5. Service Details

**Event Types You Serve:**
Select all applicable:
- [ ] Weddings
- [ ] Corporate events
- [ ] Birthday parties
- [ ] Festivals
- [ ] School events
- [ ] Fundraisers
- [ ] Public events
- [ ] Private parties
- [ ] Holiday events

**Service Styles:**
- Individual orders (guests choose from menu)
- Pre-set menu (same for everyone)
- Buffet style
- Family style
- Plated service
- Combination

**Capacity:**
- Meals per hour you can serve
- Max guests per event
- Ideal event size
- Multiple trucks available (if applicable)

**Equipment & Requirements:**

**What You Need from Venue:**
- [ ] Power (specify voltage/amps)
- [ ] Water hookup
- [ ] Drainage
- [ ] Level ground
- [ ] Specific clearances (height, width)

**What You Provide:**
- [ ] Self-contained (generator, water tank)
- [ ] Serving staff
- [ ] Plates/utensils
- [ ] Napkins
- [ ] Condiments
- [ ] Trash removal
- [ ] Tables/tent (if applicable)

**Setup Requirements:**
- Space needed (e.g., "20ft x 10ft")
- Setup time required (e.g., "1 hour before service")
- Breakdown time (e.g., "30 minutes after")
- Access requirements (loading dock, gate width, etc.)

**Staffing:**
- Number of staff included
- Additional staff available (extra cost)
- What staff will do (cook, serve, clean)

### 6. Availability & Calendar

**Operating Schedule:**
- Days of the week you work
- Typical hours
- Seasonal variations
- Holiday schedule

**Booking Windows:**
- **Minimum Advance Notice**: "2 weeks required"
- **Maximum Advance Booking**: "6 months ahead"
- **Lead Time Preferences**: "Prefer 1 month notice for large events"

**Calendar Setup:**
1. Click "Manage Calendar"
2. Block out unavailable dates
3. Mark special events you're already booked for
4. Set recurring blackout dates (every Sunday, etc.)
5. Update regularly

**Busy Seasons:**
- Note your high-demand periods
- Wedding season (spring/summer)
- Holiday events (Nov-Dec)
- Festival circuit
- Helps customers book early

**Last-Minute Bookings:**
- Do you accept them?
- Additional fees?
- Minimum notice for rush bookings?

### 7. Policies & Requirements

**Cancellation Policy:**
Use Fleet Feast standard or customize:
- 7+ days: 100% refund
- 3-6 days: 50% refund
- <3 days: No refund

**Or Create Custom:**
- Define your specific terms
- Must be fair to customers
- Clear and unambiguous

**Modification Policy:**
- How customers can request changes
- Deadline for changes
- Fees for changes (if any)

**Weather Policy:**
- What happens in bad weather?
- Covered service only?
- Rain plan required?
- Cancellation terms

**Venue Requirements:**
- Must have permits for trucks
- Adequate parking/access
- Power/water availability
- Level ground required
- No HOA restrictions

**Alcohol Policy:**
- Do you serve alcohol? (requires license)
- BYOB allowed?
- Any restrictions?

**Dietary Accommodations:**
- Vegan options available
- Gluten-free options
- Allergy accommodations
- Advance notice required for special requests

**Payment Terms:**
- When deposit is due (upon booking)
- When final payment is due (72 hours before)
- Accepted payment methods
- Gratuity policy (included or separate)

### 8. Social Media & Links

**Connect Your Presence:**
- Facebook page
- Instagram handle
- Twitter account
- TikTok (if applicable)
- Website URL
- YouTube channel

**Why Link Social:**
- Builds trust
- Shows activity and engagement
- Customer testimonials visible
- More photos/videos
- Behind-the-scenes content

**Reviews & Testimonials:**
- Import from other platforms
- Showcase best reviews
- Video testimonials
- Customer quotes

## Optimizing Your Profile

### Search Engine Optimization (SEO)

**Keywords:**
Include relevant terms naturally:
- Your cuisine type
- Your city/region
- Special dietary options
- Event types you specialize in
- Unique offerings

**Example:**
"Austin BBQ food truck specializing in Texas-style brisket,
wedding catering, corporate events, and festival service.
Gluten-free and vegetarian options available."

### Make It Scannable

**Use Formatting:**
- Short paragraphs
- Bullet points
- Bold key information
- Headers for sections
- White space

**Highlight Important Info:**
- Awards
- Years in business
- Signature dishes
- Service area
- Minimum/maximum capacities

### Call-to-Action

**End With Invitation:**
"Ready to book Rollin' Smoke BBQ for your event? Click 'Request
Booking' to get started, or message us with questions. We can't
wait to serve you!"

### Keep It Updated

**Regular Updates:**
- New menu items
- Seasonal specials
- Fresh photos
- Updated availability
- New events/awards

**At Least Quarterly:**
- Review all information for accuracy
- Update photos (seasonal shots)
- Refresh description if needed
- Add new testimonials

## Profile Checklist

Use this to ensure completeness:

**Business Info:**
- [ ] Business name and tagline
- [ ] Accurate cuisine types
- [ ] Service area defined
- [ ] Contact info current

**Photos:**
- [ ] Cover photo uploaded
- [ ] Logo uploaded
- [ ] 10+ truck photos
- [ ] 20+ food photos
- [ ] 5+ event photos
- [ ] High quality, well-lit, professional

**About:**
- [ ] Business description (200-500 words)
- [ ] Story is compelling
- [ ] Unique selling points highlighted
- [ ] Personal touches included

**Menu:**
- [ ] Current menu uploaded
- [ ] All items have descriptions
- [ ] Prices clearly listed
- [ ] Dietary info included
- [ ] Photos for signature items

**Service Details:**
- [ ] Event types selected
- [ ] Service style described
- [ ] Capacity/limits noted
- [ ] Equipment needs listed
- [ ] What's included clarified

**Availability:**
- [ ] Operating days/hours set
- [ ] Calendar updated
- [ ] Booking windows defined
- [ ] Blackout dates marked

**Policies:**
- [ ] Cancellation policy set
- [ ] Payment terms clear
- [ ] Requirements listed
- [ ] Special accommodations noted

**Social/Links:**
- [ ] Social media connected
- [ ] Website linked (if applicable)
- [ ] Reviews showcased

## Profile Preview

**Before Publishing:**
1. Click "Preview Profile"
2. See how customers view it
3. Check on mobile and desktop
4. Test all links
5. Proofread for typos

**Get Feedback:**
- Ask a friend or family member
- What's clear? What's confusing?
- Does it make them want to book?
- Any missing information?

## After Setup

### Monitor Performance

**Track Metrics:**
- Profile views
- Booking request rate
- Conversion rate
- Response time
- Customer reviews

**Adjust Based on Data:**
- Low views? Improve SEO and photos
- Low conversion? Clarify pricing or add details
- Slow response? Set up auto-replies

### A/B Testing

**Try Different Approaches:**
- Test different cover photos
- Vary your description
- Adjust pricing presentation
- See what converts better

### Continuous Improvement

**Never "Done":**
- Always room to improve
- Learn from competitors
- Study high-performing profiles
- Adapt to trends

**Stay Fresh:**
- Seasonal photos
- Holiday specials
- New menu items
- Updated testimonials

## Common Profile Mistakes

**What Hurts Your Profile:**

❌ **Poor Quality Photos**: Blurry, dark, unprofessional
❌ **Incomplete Information**: Missing prices, policies, details
❌ **Generic Description**: Sounds like everyone else
❌ **No Personality**: Boring, corporate-speak
❌ **Outdated Info**: Old menu, wrong contact info
❌ **Too Much Text**: Giant walls of text, no formatting
❌ **Spelling/Grammar Errors**: Looks unprofessional
❌ **Vague Pricing**: "Contact for quote" on everything
❌ **Missing Photos**: Customers can't visualize your offering
❌ **No Social Proof**: No reviews, testimonials, or credentials

**What Makes Profiles Shine:**

✅ **Professional Photos**: Clear, appetizing, high-res
✅ **Complete Details**: Everything answered upfront
✅ **Compelling Story**: Unique angle, personal connection
✅ **Clear Pricing**: Transparent, easy to understand
✅ **Regular Updates**: Fresh content, current info
✅ **Mobile-Friendly**: Looks good on phones
✅ **Social Proof**: Reviews, awards, testimonials
✅ **Easy to Contact**: Clear CTAs, quick response
✅ **Personality**: Authentic voice, not generic
✅ **Organized**: Easy to scan, find information
